abstract: The erosion of high-end fashion brands by fast-fashion copycats (e.g., Zara, H&M) has stirred controversies and unceasing legal attempts to copyright fashion designs. Despite the purported negative impact of copycats, the effect of fashion copycats on high-end brands remains empirically unclear. Research on this topic has been impeded by the absence of a modeling framework to quantify fashion and by the lack of consumer-level data on fashion choices. The authors collect data on the posting behaviors of consumers on a fashion-specific social media platform and develop a dynamic structural model with deep learning image analytics to characterize consumers’ choices of brands and styles. Results suggest that fast-fashion copycats can both harm high-end brands (a cannibalization effect) and help them (a market expansion effect). The authors also identify both static and dynamic mechanisms that contribute to the market expansion effect: The affordability of mixing copycats with high-end brands boosts the number of high-end items featured in posts by financially constrained consumers (a static mechanism). In addition, good styles from copycats enable users to build their popularity on social media over time, which may increase the users' valuation of high-end brands and reduce the users' future costs via sponsorship opportunities (dynamic mechanisms). The results could inform policy makers about the potential consequences of prohibiting fashion copycats.

ob.ai_reason: 使用深度学习图像分析量化时尚选择，属AI方法应用；快时尚对高端品牌影响属营销核心议题
ob.tldr: 通过时尚社交媒体发帖行为数据结合深度学习图像分析构建动态结构模型，发现快时尚仿制既会侵蚀高端品牌（cannibalization），也能扩大市场（market expansion）。机制包括：低价混搭提升预算受限用户的奢侈品曝光；优质仿款提升用户社交媒体人气，进而提高其对高端品牌的价值认知并通过赞助降低未来成本。

title: How Do Fast-Fashion Copycats Affect the Popularity of Premium Brands? Evidence from Social Media
title_zh: 快时尚模仿者如何影响高端品牌知名度？来自社交媒体的证据
